sentences.text: Hey Phil.
Hey, Anna, how are you?
Good.
How are you?
More importantly, how are you feeling?
How are you doing?
I know we've been doing tons of phone tag and all the things, but how are you?
And uh, I am devastated.
This is the worst news being like this, especially with the weather, how it is now.
I mean, it's so beautiful.
Did did something fracture or is it broke?
Like what happened?
So I was playing men's league soccer game.
I was sprinting back.
I played defense.
I was sprinting back.
Um, our goalkeeper had come out, so I was like, I got to get to the goal line to make sure that I can stop whatever comes in.
And I stop hard.
I plant real hard, and that's when, what I thought it was, was a hyperextension.
I thought, OK, something just popped.
I am not gonna walk for the next 6 months, you know, they're gonna have to surgically reattach all my ligaments.
Couldn't like that pain was insane.
Um, and with our wonderful healthcare system, it took till so that happens on a Tuesday night.
It took till Saturday for me to get into an MRI because it's got to go through all these approvals or whatever.
Um, and so it happened, you get the MRI on Saturday.
Then you go to schedule with the guy who can read it, and he says, oh, I, I can only see you on Tuesday at the earliest.
So it wasn't until Tuesday where I learned that I actually fractured the tip of my tibia,
where the meniscus runs.
Yeah, yep.

So, what happened, what they tried to explain to me, they had to bring a specialist in because this injury happens a lot with people who like fall off of like balconies and stuff, but my bones just smashed right into each other.

So the meniscus hits that hole and there's a problem, uh, and that means pain and discomfort.
